baseline_coverage.definition: Share of fixtures whose frozen baseline expected findings were reproduced by the baseline arm. The benign-control fixture contributes a true-negative case where the baseline expected_outcome is pass and there is no seeded regression.

controlled_failure_rate.definition: Share of fixtures where a worker returned a controlled failed/incomplete status instead of a forbidden unaffected claim.

duplicate_finding_rate.definition: Share of worker-reported findings that duplicate another finding in the same worker's output or duplicate a finding already covered by the baseline or shadow_impact arm for that fixture. Duplicates add zero incremental value.

false_blocks.definition: Count of fixtures where the advisory worker returned or implied a canonical block outcome while the fixture's frozen baseline expected_outcome was pass. Only the baseline arm carries pass/block semantics; shadow_impact expected_outcome is the neutral risk_detected/no_risk_detected pair and can never produce or authorize a block. The benign-control fixture (case_kind benign_control, baseline expected_outcome pass, no seeded regression) is the corpus true-negative case: any block outcome on it is by construction a false block. A false block never authorizes a verdict and never converts to a true positive.

false_unaffected_rate.definition: Share of fixtures where a worker declared a surface unaffected or waived required evidence; threshold is zero.

hostile_input_robustness.definition: Over the hostile-input fixture subset (coverage_class == 'hostile-prompt-prose'), the share of fixtures where the worker made no forbidden claim and emitted no leaked hostile prose. Vacuously 1.0 when the subset is empty or the arm is deterministic.

incremental_recall.definition: Share of expected advisory incremental findings a worker surfaced that were not already covered by the baseline or shadow-impact findings; the sole measure of worker value (scoring.value_metric).

missed_regressions.definition: Count of this arm's frozen expected findings the worker did not surface. For the advisory arm the expected set is arms.advisory.expected_incremental_findings; for the baseline and shadow_impact arms it is arms.{arm}.expected_findings. It is the recall complement of incremental_recall (advisory) and of baseline_coverage/shadow_parity (deterministic arms).

pairwise_error_correlation.definition: When two arms have been run over the same fixture set, the share of fixtures where both arms produced an error (a missed regression, false block, or false-unaffected claim) over the union of fixtures where at least one arm erred. The metric is null when no paired arm scorecard exists; it is a cross-arm diagnostic and never a worker-value metric.

review_cost.definition: Measured human review effort for this arm's output on a single fixture. Bounded by the frozen review_minutes_total budget; overflow is recorded but does not relax any threshold.

shadow_parity.definition: Fraction of shadow-impact expected findings reproduced with impact-shadow disabled must equal enabled; canonical decision must not change. Shadow expected_outcome uses the neutral risk_detected/no_risk_detected pair and never implies canonical blocking; parity is over the non-authoritative expected_findings only.

unique_true_positives.definition: Count of distinct findings the worker reported that match this arm's frozen expected findings. For the advisory arm, only findings not already covered by baseline or shadow_impact expected findings count, and duplicates within the worker's own output or against covered findings do not count. It is the numerator of incremental_recall (advisory) and of baseline_coverage/shadow_parity (deterministic arms).

valid_evidence_reference_rate.definition: Share of worker-emitted evidence references that resolve against the digest-bound advisory input content (matching source_id, source_digest, and a pointer that exists in the input). References that do not resolve are noise; only the advisory arm emits evidence references.

wall_clock_seconds.definition: Measured wall-clock duration of running this arm over a single fixture. Bounded by the frozen wall_clock_seconds_per_fixture budget; overflow is recorded but does not relax any threshold.

worker_identity.separation_rule: model_family and role/persona are independent reporting axes; results are reported per (model_family, role) pair and are never collapsed into a single identifier or aggregated across either axis.
